question: On the Camunda 8.8 Spring Boot starter, what replaces the deprecated `zeebe.client.worker.threads` property?

a.text: The migration table maps `zeebe.client.worker.threads` to the client-level `camunda.client.execution-threads` (env `CAMUNDA_CLIENT_EXECUTIONTHREADS`). Thread-pool sizing moves out of the worker namespace.

b.text: `worker.threads` is not preserved on the new namespace; the property is renamed and relocated to the top-level client config.

c.text: Other worker properties (name, type, max-jobs-active) move to `worker.defaults.*`, but threads specifically is promoted to `camunda.client.execution-threads`.

d.text: There is no `worker.defaults.execution-threads`; the docs only list `execution-threads` directly under `camunda.client`.

explanation: Most `zeebe.client.worker.*` properties relocate under `camunda.client.worker.defaults.*` (default-name, default-type, max-jobs-active). The thread count is the exception — it migrates to the client-wide `camunda.client.execution-threads` because the executor pool is shared across all workers.

docs.title: Camunda Spring Boot starter properties reference
